name: Huntington Coach Corp
owner_or_parent: Beacon Mobility (Audax Private Equity portfolio company)
fleet_size_est: 900+ (combined Beacon Long Island platform: Huntington Coach, Huntington Coach LLC, Hendrickson Bus, Dell Transportation)
districts_or_customers: Long Island school districts; one of the largest providers on Long Island; runs Buster the Bus safety education program in partner districts
recent_news: Feb 2023 - Huntington Coach Corp, Huntington Coach LLC, Hendrickson Bus and Dell Transportation joined Beacon Mobility (Audax PE) as the Long Island platform.
fit_notes: NOT a tuck-in candidate - already owned by Beacon Mobility (Audax PE), a direct Summit competitor. Useful for competitive intel on Long Island consolidation only.

name: Handi Van Inc
districts_or_customers: Miami-Dade special needs/wheelchair clientele; no specific public school district contracts confirmed
recent_news: No material M&A, growth, or litigation news identified in recent searches; minimal web footprint beyond directory listings.
fit_notes: Small Miami-Dade paratransit/wheelchair operator (~$3.9M rev). Not core yellow-bus, but a possible SPED/NEMT bolt-on if Summit pursues Florida special-needs expansion. Low confidence on ownership succession; needs direct outreach. Note address overlap with several Miami wheelchair/paratransit operators - validate the entity.

name: Carrier Coach Inc
districts_or_customers: Historically served Western NY / Lake Shore-area districts before closure
recent_news: Carrier Coach closed after 40+ years citing lost contracts and competition; dozens of buses/vans auctioned on Lakeshore Rd, Hamburg (WIVB report). Corporate domain carriercoach.com is parked for sale on HugeDomains, confirming inactive status.
fit_notes: NOT a viable target - company appears to have wound down; fleet liquidated and domain abandoned. Drop from active pipeline. Worth flagging to confirm whether any successor entity exists in the Buffalo / WNY market (e.g., WNY Bus Co.).

name: Royal Coach Lines
owner_or_parent: Beacon Mobility (Audax Private Equity portfolio company)
principals: Rosa Ibacache (Operations per source data); pre-acquisition family ownership not publicly identified
fleet_size_est: large - among largest school transportation contractors in Westchester County; multi-yard operation across Yonkers, Elmsford, Greenburgh, Ossining, Somers, Thornwood
districts_or_customers: Westchester County school districts including City of Yonkers; Greenwich Japanese School; broad tri-state charter customer base
recent_news: Sep 1, 2023 - Beacon Mobility (Audax PE) acquired Royal Coach Lines (Foley & Lardner advised Beacon).
fit_notes: NOT a tuck-in candidate - acquired by Beacon Mobility (Audax PE) in Sept 2023. Together with Huntington Coach, signals Beacon's aggressive NY metro roll-up and Summit's primary competitor footprint in Westchester/Long Island.

name: F & S Transportation
employees_est: 60 (per source); 88 drivers per FMCSA, plus 100+ part-time drivers per company materials
districts_or_customers: Multiple school districts across York and Adams Counties, PA (e.g., Northeastern School District driver-recruitment posting); also operates F&S Transportation MD Inc. affiliate suggesting MD cross-border work
recent_news: FMCSA 'Satisfactory' compliance rating (Jan 2016 - last public rating); active recruitment for drivers; no public M&A news.
fit_notes: Strong Summit fit - family-owned PA contractor (~$3.4M rev, ~136 buses) in York/Adams Counties with established multi-district contract base and MD cross-border affiliate. Founded 1976 (~50 years) suggests possible founder/family succession opening. Geographic adjacency to Summit's PA platform makes this a credible tuck-in.
